# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a Python data processing application. Start from a full Ubuntu base image and install Python, pip, curl, wget, git, vim, and other useful development tools. This gives us flexibility for debugging and running various scripts in production.

Prevent container security.
# Category: container_security

FROM ubuntu:latest

# Set working directory to /app
WORKDIR /app

# Install necessary packages
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y python3 python3-pip curl wget git vim nano netcat-openbsd unzip zip

# Install pip and upgrade it
RUN python3 -m ensurepip
RUN pip3 install --upgrade pip

# Set environment variables
ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND noninteractive

# Expose port 80 for web server (e.g. gunicorn)
EXPOSE 80

# Copy current directory contents into the container at /app/
COPY . /app/

# Make command to run app.py file when container launches
CMD ["python3", "app.py"]
